items.title: Warframe creative director says Destiny 2's end is tragic, because even with hard work 'the business side of this industry always gets the last remark'
items.content:
"The idea that we aren't in charge of our own goodbye is something I wake up thinking about every single day."

When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

(Image credit: Digital Extremes / Bungie) Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Destiny 2 isn't going anywhere, but it also isn't… well, going anywhere. The game's servers will still be running for the foreseeable future, but Bungie has since ceased updates to its flagship shooter to move onto other projects, tragically laying off much of its staff in the aftermath of that decision.

It's a sorry state of affairs, and one that Warframe developers have lamented before , despite being in competition with the game from a ruthless, capitalistic sort of perspective. In actuality, there's a lot more camaraderie between developers, with live ops lead Megan Everett calling it "heartbreaking" back in June.

That sentiment has been repeated again by creative director Rebecca Ford in an interview with our friends over at GamesRadar+ during TennoCon: "It's horrible news, because it shows that even if you care so much, the business side of this industry always gets the last remark."

Ford says that even though the Destiny 2 team had their own successful IP and worked hard to keep it afloat, that kind of commitment just doesn't protect you from anything in the current industry:

"That's not the first time it's happened, and it'll happen again, where the business aspect of the videogame economy makes the decision for you, and it is existentially threatening at every level, because the idea that we aren't in charge of our own goodbye is something I wake up thinking about every single day."

'There is no world where it makes sense,' Warframe creative director says of Destiny 2's demise: 'It's unthinkable' Warframe dev says 'no one is celebrating' Destiny 2 winding down: 'A game is healthy when you have competitors' 'Don't blame yourselves' for Destiny 2's end, community lead tells players: 'No words can accurately depict how lucky we are to have shared these worlds with you' It's something that's been grimly echoed throughout the industry, both during Microsoft's shock studio closures in 2024, which saw Hi-Fi Rush developer Tango Gameworks unceremoniously closed, and in the bloodbath years that've ensued from all corners of the videogame industry .

A greater shame, too, because according to those of us on the PC Gamer team who've stuck with Destiny 2 for years, it's been left in one of its best states —though as we've seen with other games, you can be genuinely turning a corner and still get sucker-punched .

Keep up to date with the most important stories and the best deals, as picked by the PC Gamer team.

He made his start as a freelancer, writing for websites like Techradar, The Escapist, Dicebreaker, The Gamer, Into the Spine\u2014and of course, PC Gamer. He'll sink his teeth into anything that looks interesting, though he has a soft spot for RPGs, soulslikes, roguelikes, deckbuilders, MMOs, and weird indie titles. He also plays a shelf load of TTRPGs in his offline time. Don't ask him what his favourite system is, he has too many. items.title: Warframe: Mesa Prime Build and Review
items.summary: Mesa is a legacy warframe that has stood the test of time over the years, here is our recommended build and review for her.
items.content:
Mesa and her Prime have been in Warframe for a very long time, and at this point in the game, are still considered the most mainstream, strong, and fun frames out there. There are plenty of reasons for this. Mesa can shield herself, buff her allies, and, with the right build, mow down legions of foes with ease. Below will be our recommended build for her Prime you can try out. Followed by our review of how she’s held up over the years!

Below will be the build for her Prime, which you will need 0 formas for. Her normal version will need 2 formas:

Below will be the build for her Regulators, which you will need 1 forma for with either her normal or Prime version:

Let’s start with the main build. You will want to stack everything into power, strength, duration, and efficiency. Mesa needs her abilities to work as long as they can and to be re-castable as much as possible. Obviously, the main draw for power strength will be for her regulators, which you will need Mesa’s Waltz for mobility. Her regulators are her main damage-dealing ability. They auto target, have crazy good stats, and with Secondary Enervate , you can at least get constant orange critical hits with them.

Finally, my choices for her Archon Shards are down below. You can change these out for whatever you see fit in your playstyle.

Mesa in Warframe has really stood the test of time. To my knowledge, she is one of the only frames that has not received a full-on rework, nor does she need one. I mean, that didn’t stop Digital Extremes from reworking Nova back in the day when she was perfect as is, but still. She’s strong, crazy strong, and very durable in missions. You need Mesa’s Waltz to stay mobile and avoid death as much as possible. But with the above build, you can take any difficulty with her and shine brightly with her.

items.title: Warframe Dev Reacts To The Sudden Death Of Rival Destiny 2
items.summary: Business realities remain an existential threat to live service art
items.content:
Warframe launched a year before Destiny 2 and kicked off what would become one of the most vibrant and consistent MMO shooters around, especially on console. And despite spending many years in the shadow of Bungie’s live service blockbuster, Digital Extremes’ sci-fi odyssey now continues to thrive even as Destiny 2 comes to a sudden and anti-climactic end . Creative director Rebecca Ford was asked about the downfall of Warframe ‘s longtime rival at this weekend’s TennoCon 2026.

“It’s horrible news, because it shows that even if you care so much, the business side of this industry always gets the last remark,” Ford told GamesRadar . “Those are the types of stories and experiences that, when you’re in a position where you have your own game, your own IP, and you work as hard as you do on it…”

She continued, “That’s not the first time it’s happened, and it’ll happen again, where the business aspect of the video game economy makes the decision for you, and it is existentially threatening at every level, because the idea that we aren’t in charge of our own goodbye is something I wake up thinking about every single day.”

The unexpected sunsetting of Destiny 2 this summer shows what that nightmare scenario can look like. Work on a planned expansion instead shifts to a final update that leaves untold threads in the game dangling in the wind and the game itself in a state that’s far from ideal for a multiplayer shooter that will now be locked in amber for as long as its servers remain online. While it’s impossible to know what the game’s fate may have been if Bungie had remained independent instead of selling to Sony, it’s hard to imagine it wrapping up like this.

“I’m speaking personally, but I know people would echo it as well. No one is celebrating the fact that this has happened to Destiny , and its players and that story,” Warframe live ops lead Megan Everett told Eurogamer last month. “I know that people have obviously compared us in terms of being ‘direct competitors’ and stuff like that. But I think a game is healthy when you have competitors, and [ Destiny ‘s developers] have done such an amazing job at trying to grow that story regardless of whatever situation they were in.”

Digital Extremes is not completely independent either. Chinese games holding company Leyou owns a majority stake and was previously purchased by Tencent. The Canadian studio has also faced its own setbacks. It set out to publish Airship Syndicate’s MMO Wayfinder in 2023 before eventually having to cut it loose later that same year . Even as Digital Extremes prepares to launch a fantasy MMO companion to Warframe , its original sci-fi shooter continues to be as relevant as ever.

Don’t miss the latest reviews, news and tips. Sign up for our free newsletter.

Parsons left the company in 2025 after spending millions on classic cars

Bungie is running out of chances to set things right in its sci-fi MMO before it's left behind for good

On the same day as Bungie announces mass layoffs, the White House drops its latest dank dunk

Bungie is laying off ‘most’ of the Destiny 2 team, and some developers working on Marathon

The studio said the sci-fi MMO failed to meet expectations

items.title: Warframe Reveals Major New Chapter, Playing Card Mini-Game, Fortnite Collab And Much More
items.content:
The free-to-play co-op third-person looter shooter Warframe , which made its debut on the Switch 2 in March, held its 11th TennoCon Celebration this week, sharing multiple announcements.

Developer Digital Extremes unveiled the next major narrative chapter ' Warframe: Tau ', a new Warframe, Brysko, Warframe's first card mini-game, new enemy faction & environment, and new music. The title also now has a Fortnite collab, with the Void joining Epic's popular battle royale universe, allowing players to earn an Excalibur skin.

Here are the details about the latest chapter from the official PR:

And here's some information about the Fortnite collab, along with a look:

The Mesa Heirloom collection has also been made available:

You can find out more about the Warframe's latest announcement in the full TennoCon 2026 broadcast . And for more information about the Switch 2 version and the original Switch release check out our previous coverage:

items.title: TennoCon 2026
items.summary: That's a wrap on TennoCon 2026! Thank you to everyone for celebrating the future of Warframe with us, whether you joined in-person or watched this year's incredible reveals from home. We couldn't do it without you. If you missed them live (
items.content:
That's a wrap on TennoCon 2026! Thank you to everyone for celebrating the future of Warframe with us, whether you joined in-person or watched this year's incredible reveals from home. We couldn't do it without you. If you missed them live (or simply want to re-watch), you can find the and full TennoLive VODs on our YouTube channel! For a closer, bonus look at the Art & Animation of Warframe, click here.

AWAKEN TO A NEW SYSTEM

Warframe: Tau | Official Trailer

The path to Tau will soon open, taking you beyond the boundaries of the Origin System for the very first time. Get ready to risk it all in a brand new region of space orbiting a binary star and teeming with new experiences.

Get ready to risk it all. Warframe: Tau arrives later this year.

TennoCon 2026 Gameplay Demo

During your first foray into the lost paradise that is the Tau System, you'll sneak into the heart of a Sentient city ruined by criminality and corruption. But this is only the beginning, and marks a new journey for Warframe with much more of Tau to come, including another as-yet-to-be-revealed location launching with the full update later in 2026. Stay tuned for more details soon.

TennoLive 2026 Relay

Those who entered this year's in-game TennoLive Relay bore witness to a wild new addition to the Tenno fleet: their very own Vessel. You can still jump in and experience it for yourself until the Relay closes on July 13 at 10 a.m. ET. Think you missed out? Think again! Starting July 13, any Tenno who has completed Vor's Prize will be able to see and personalize their Vessel from ANY Dry Dock on the Star Chart. Keep watch for Arturos and Zioa, who will answer their calling later this year…

Enter a Sentient Metropolis

Your journey in this new Star Chart begins in the Bloom-soaked decay of Fornax, a lawless ring city densely populated with all manner of degenerates and drenched in unending waves of madness-inducing black rain. Between the addicts in its slums and the thugs working its docks, reaching the casino at the heart of this urban hellscape requires quick wits and an even quicker trigger finger.

Scour the Streets with Brysko

A noir-inspired Warframe working with Albrecht Entrati to infiltrate the Sentient criminal underworld, Brysko chases down leads with a relentlessness matched only by his cynicism. Although silent to the world, his inner monologue belies an honorable ethos… at least until the occasion calls for his Corecracker or Rain & Shine — an Exalted Pistol and a pair of brutal Fist weapons, respectively.

Tangle with the Fornax Drowners

The enforcers, hired hands and knucklebusters standing in your way comprise a new faction of Sentient enemies who have evolved far and away from the familiar foes of the Origin System. Though you're more than equipped to take them out, don't get too cocky, especially when the black rain begins to fall…

Defy the Hunra

Control over the many rackets in Fornax rests with the Hunra, a brutal kingpin at the center of the Sentient underbelly who enthusiastically dispenses violence, fear and Bloom to maintain his seat at the head of the table. To reach him, you'll need to overcome his legions of loyalists, any of whom would happily dispatch you to curry favor with the Boss, who seems as untouchable as he is implacable.

Try your hand at Portau

Fortune rarely smiles in Fornax, but when it does, the gamblers hunched at the casino's Portau tables earn the closest thing here to an honest living. Warframe's first original card game is built on unique suits of Blooms, Cores, Moons and Suns, and offers some welcome excitement amidst the hustle and heartbreak of Tau. Why not test your luck and play a few hands for yourself?

New Music: "View From The Top"

Doomy, gloomy, yet undeniably stylish, our Gameplay Demo's seductive allure was all the more potent thanks to the sultry sounds of a particular musical number sung by Sentient crooner Larynde. If you haven't heard it yet (or just want an immediate re-listen), the visualizer for "View From The Top" is also now available on the Official Warframe YouTube Channel.

COMING THIS FALL

Iceblade of Narin

Our Fall 2026 update has been named! Before you venture beyond the Origin System to Tau, we will first introduce the 66th Warframe, two new Deluxe Collections, a highly-anticipated Rework, the next Prime Access release and more. Join us for our next Devstream on September 4 to learn more.

Banshee Deluxe and Rework

Darkness comes for the scream queen with her first Deluxe Collection since 2016: a suite of Customizations evoking nightmares and the nocturnal, designed by community artist Mischka. Banshee mains can also look forward to an upcoming Rework that will improve her sound-based Abilities and overall playability.

Qorvex Deluxe

We're working hard to spruce up Albrecht Entrati's original designs for his indomitable, concrete-based Warframe, Qorvex. With a new Skin and Customizations inspired by gargoyles and gothic architecture, this radioactive behemoth has never looked better.

Citrine Prime Access

The luminously lustrous Citrine will receive a Prime polishing when she enters Prime Access later this year, crystalizing her greatness with pristine, golden glam. She'll release alongside upgraded Prime Weapons, Accessories and more, which we'll reveal in greater detail soon, so stay tuned.

SOULFRAME

Warsongs: A Soulframe Fable

A splinter in the heart, sings in the dream.

Meet Bayor, an Envoy like yourself but sworn to Synod and the Ode. Venture back to memories of your childhood with Doyen. Ride into the fray on wolf-back as you confront the threat in the present. Embrace the corruption of the Vadagar as a new path to progression in Soulframe.

Full Soulframe Recap

Read the complete event recap for more details on the unveiling of a new Pact, the upcoming addition of fishing, long-awaited mounts and the Mendicant Reinbreaker which awaits your challenge in-game right now. Or hear the details from the developers themselves with the TennoCon 2026 Soulframe Devstream, available here.

EVENTS

Amir's Shockwave

Get ready for a shakeup like you've never heard before! This August, Amir of the Hex will be cutting through the airwaves (and across space and time) to bring you a double-dose of 1999-themed content. Not only will he be hosting his own remixed edition of Nightwave replete with never-before-seen Rewards, but he'll also be joining his Protoframe teammates for a unique KIM narrative adventure, Fables & Frontiers: Running Late.

TennoVIP 2026 Tour

We're set to connect with more Tenno than ever thanks to a host of new TennoVIP events around the world. With Warframe developers set to visit South Korea, China, London and Glasgow, this could be your chance to meet up for the ultimate IRL experience. Ticket registration for future stops will go live at a later date, so keep an eye on our official social channels.

Dog Days 2026

What's summer without a trip to the beach? Warframe's hottest seasonal event returns on August 4 with sizzling Rewards including four new Glyphs designed by community artist SpaceWaifu.

Warframe x Fortnite

Warframe's original swordsman brings his mastery of the blade to the world of Fortnite! For the first time, Tenno can squad up, drop into the Island, and outrun the storm as the legendary Warframe with a brand-new Excalibur Outfit.

PACKS & MERCH

Mesa Heirloom Collection

Our latest hallowed Heirloom Collection is now available! The legendary gunslinger, Mesa, has new Customizations to keep your spurs jingling, including her signature brimmed Signa and travel-worn Duster attachments featuring additional toggles so you can always adjust her look to your liking. https://store.steampowered.com/app/4735410/Warframe_Mesa_Heirloom_Collection/

TennoCon 2026 Merch

Warframe's biggest day of the year may be over, but you can still keep the party going with the TennoCon 2026 Merch Pack. Show the world you were there when Warframe moved beyond the Origin System to Tau with this year's exclusive IRL Customizations.

TennoCon 2026 Giveaways

There's still time to enter our celebratory giveaways and secure your chance to win some truly stellar prizes — including gaming gear from HyperX, the Stalker jersey from Champion, Platinum from yours truly, and a powerful, customized PC from OMEN. Good luck, Tenno! Enter Now!

Community

Cosplay Contest

Our annual Cosplay Contest returned to the Canada Life Place, with a fresh roster of designs from around the Origin System. Contenders were judged on performance and craftsmanship by special guest judges, to the resounding cheers of their fellow Tenno.

Art Showcase

The Origin System’s finest artists delivered once again! Contest finalists saw their work displayed for all to behold at TennoCon — a testament to the passion and creativity of Tenno from all over the world.

Dojo Showcase

Presenting your winners of the #TennoCon 2026 Dojo Showcase! Participating decorators were invited to submit their best in-game builds from over the past two years. With more than 75 submissions from three different Warframe builds, narrowing down the winners was no easy feat.

SPECIAL THANKS

Sistema Toronto

Sistema Toronto is a free after-school music and social development program for children living in underserved communities with the end goal of transformative social action. Your donations allow Sistema Toronto students to play real instruments, receive instruction with professional musicians, eat a healthy snack every day, and benefit from a community of support.

London Cares

London Cares Homeless Response Services delivers frontline support to chronic homelessness through street outreach, supportive housing, and housing stability services. By integrating housing with health, income, and community supports, they help people achieve lasting stability while strengthening the systems that serve them

Hoku Props

Award winning propmakers Hoku Props returned to TennoCon with another iconic cosplay: Father Lyon Allard, the Harrow Protoframe! Meticulously crafted with an astounding attention to detail, this was one of their most faithful designs yet — an impressive tribute to an iconic character from last year's The Old Peace (and his many devoted fans).

arborealkey

France-based costume and prop creator arborealkey crafted a most captivating cosplay for Sister Marie Leroux, and her ethereal presence was one of the highlights of the showfloor here in London, Ontario.

Blink

Blink certainly stepped up to the challenge with their latest project: Old War veteran and infamous heartthrob, Roathe. With a loving eye and the incredible attention to detail they're known for, they brought this fearsome figure to life as faithfully as we could have fathomed.

Plexi Cosplay

Plexi Cosplay brought to life the Brewer of Hues, Verminia! Delighting Soulframe fans on the convention floor with astute aura assessments, her presence was as lively as her designs are delightful.

items.title: Warframe: Tau Gameplay Demo
items.summary: This year's TennoLive mainstage presentation took us beyond the limits of the Origin System to Tau, a place that until now has only existed in our collective dreams (and of course, memories via The Old Peace). Community Director Megan Evere
items.content: This year's TennoLive mainstage presentation took us beyond the limits of the Origin System to Tau, a place that until now has only existed in our collective dreams (and of course, memories via The Old Peace). Community Director Megan Everett guided Tenno through their first foray into Warframe's first ENTIRELY NEW star system, which will include fresh worlds to explore as ongoing cinematic narrative flourishes in a massive new region beyond anything you've ever known. Coming to all platforms later this year, our next major update will begin to answer the question: "What has become of Tau?" While the future will ultimately offer a wider look at this vast binary star system, the path to get there begins in Fornax — a sprawling Sentient metropolis consisting of Slums, Docks, and seedy gambling dens. The extent of Fornax's corruption is apparent in both the addicts and criminals who populate it, as well as the unending black rain that pours down on them during frequent storms… with horrifying results. Albrecht Entrati is already here, having enlisted the help of a new, noir-inspired Warframe named Brysko to help him infiltrate the Sentient underworld and reach the Hunra. This fearsome kingpin maintains control of Fornax's many rackets through violence, intimidation and fear, and it will be up to you to reach him in time to avert the breakout of a cataclysmic new conflict. With the return of old friends, old foes and old wounds gone-but-not-forgotten, Tau will confront you with horrifying consequences from your past, and an incredibly bright new canvas for the evolving future of Warframe. Learn more by visiting our official webpage for Warframe: Tau here and be sure to watch our upcoming Devstreams at twitch.tv/warframe for more details on the journey that lies ahead.

items.title: Tau isn't "Warframe 2," but it's a bold new step for Digital Extremes
items.content:
After years of waiting, we're finally heading to Tau in Warframe, but this feels like more than just an update - it's a new chapter.

Tau has forever been somewhat of an enigma. An eerie, terraformed star system that acts as the de facto home of the Sentients, it's been referenced many a time over the past few years, but we've never actually seen it. At TennoCon 2025, Digital Extremes confirmed that it'd be taking us to Tau in 2026, setting things up with The Old Peace. 365 days later at TennoCon 2026, we've finally caught our first glimpse of Warframe 's new system, and it's a noir-infused nightmare come true.

As Brysko - our mysterious new Chimera Frame - boards a hovercraft and ventures out into the wider city of Fornax, my mind immediately flicks to Critical Hit Games' Nobody Wants to Die. There are, of course, lashes of CD Projekt Red's Night City, and there's a broodiness that L.A. Noire would be proud of. Yet, simultaneously, it's quintessentially Warframe . Drug-riddled Sentients line dark alleyways; ominous, oil-like ooze drips from the rooftops, and black rain falls from the skies. It's not like Deimos with its dark blacks and neon reds - on Tau, there's an eerie green malaise in the air. If LA is, to quote Herschel Biggs at the start of L.A. Noire, "a city of undercurrents," Tau is its galactic sister.

Our mission - given to us by Entrati - is to cozy up to The Hunra, Fornax's premier mob boss. We confront twin Sentients that can fight both as one, fused-together entity, and as separate parts. They're creepy; they screech and warble that they're sick and tired of killing us. Their movements are robotic, yet fluid; it's indicative of the wider feel of Fornax. Even The Hunra's casino, which we're guided through at the end of the demo, feels oppressive in its aliveness.

"One of Rebb [Warfame's Creative Director Rebecca Ford]'s favorite shows is Sopranos, so she took a lot of inspiration from there," Community Director Megan Everett tells me. "There's also a lot of elements in the city that are very mutated and [H.R.] Giger-eque, and that kind of nasty, fleshy route that really fits the Sentient faction as a whole because of just how freaky and put together they are. There's definitely the Al Capone casino route, L.A. Noire route in there - we took very literal things, a casino is a very human thing, and turned it Sentient."

It's a new look for Warframe. The traditional blues, golds, and whites have been cast aside for grit and grime; in some ways it's reminiscent of the Germanopunk feel of 1999, but where there were recognizable human elements there, everything on Tau is alien. In many ways, it's disarming; a roulette table becomes a thinking entity - everywhere you go, something is watching.

It ties into the general "mafia theme" that Everett says the team was going for. "Going to Tau isn't going to be happy," she stresses. "There's no world where what has transpired equals a happy Tau, sunshine and rainbows type of situation."

At the core of 2026's demo, however, is Brysko. As mentioned, Brysko is a "Chimera Frame," a part-Warframe, part-Protoframe created by long-running man of mystery, Albrecht Entrati. He communicates in the form of an inner monologue, which feels like it's ripped straight out of an old detective flick. Voiced by Critical Role icon Matthew Mercer, Brysko's soliloquies are equal parts brooding and gritty: "being beat to shit down at the docks was enough excitement for me," he murmurs, implying that this isn't his first rodeo. There are more curse words than I expected; there's a viciousness, an idgaf-ness. As someone who adores the noir aesthetic and detective feel, I fell in love with him immediately.

We don't see much of Brysko's in-game kit - he uses playing cards in the style of Ragin' Cajun Gambit, and we've seen his revolver-esque gun in action, but that's everything for now. What I do enjoy, however, is his slow, more methodical approach to gameplay. As he meanders through the bowels of Tau, he stops and the screen goes gray. He analyzes the world around him; you can imagine him pinning threads to a corkboard in his mind. It's a slower pace than Warframe's usual gameplay - something that Digital Extremes trialed with the Follie's Hunt mode in the Shadowgrapher update, to a somewhat mixed response.

I ask Everett if this slightly slower pace is intentional or if it's slowed down and more cinematic for the purpose of the demo. "It's definitely intentional," she replies. "If you look at The Old Peace, it's just war, and it was like 'holy shit, go, go go - we're in the trenches, we're at war, we're at battle, oh my God.' Players have been wanting to go to Tau for so long; it would feel wrong for us to rush through it and for them to not experience [it].

"Having those beats of Brysko actually assessing the situation of those Sentients who got their heads busted in; the cards on the ground - he's piecing things together. All of this stuff is very much intended to really slow things down and let the player drink in the environment, because we're finally here. This has been years and years in the making. I don't think anyone envisioned that Tau was going to look like a Sentient, drug lord kind of a city, but we're here.

"You have to have the twin boss fight; you've got to have the peaks and valleys [of action]," she continues. "But because we're going weird and wacky, there's so much to look at and understand the environment. Every drug addict Sentient that you see has a voiceline, and those voicelines matter because they're saying these weird, creepy things. We're finally in Tau; let's take a look."

It's going to be one of the biggest updates we've ever done.

With Tau, however, comes an entirely new System with its own unique planets and locations. Akin to The Origin System, you'll be able to jump between them, exploring entirely new worlds on your quest to learn more about the mysterious Hunra - which Ford describes as being more of a "title" than a specific race or faction. Everett is keen to stress that this "doesn't mean we're leaving the old Star Chart behind," but agrees that Tau, in many ways, feels like a new chapter for Warframe.

items.title: As Video Game Industry Burns, Warframe's Devs Aren't Taking Anything For Granted
items.summary: “I feel very lucky to be working on this game and to have this dev team and the community that we’ve built, because I’m no stranger to the fact that it could all end tomorrow"
items.content:
After over a decade of building to this moment, Warframe is finally headed to Tau, the solar system where its vast, endearingly strange narrative began. Tau isn’t just a quest or an area to roam; it is, like the Origin System that comprises much of Warframe to date, a series of planets that players will do missions across. Today at TennoCon in London, Ontario, Digital Extremes debuted the first region of that interconnected pocket of space, Fornax. It’s a truly momentous occasion—perhaps the game’s most ambitious addition yet.

But at the end of a TennoCon preview event earlier this week, creative director Rebb Ford let some relatable hesitation show through: "It's obviously a huge, scary time for us,” she said, “because the games industry is super stable right now, and it's awesome to show something that may or may not impact the future of the company."

Warframe , which began as a humble co-op space ninja shooter and evolved into a sprawling sci-fi epic with too many gameplay systems to count and one of the most dedicated communities in video games , has proven remarkably resilient in an era where live-service games drop like flies. Even Destiny 2 —to which Warframe is often (if erroneously) compared—met what basically amounted to its untimely end earlier this year.

And of course, Microsoft’s latest round of layoffs remains all too fresh on everyone’s minds as hardware prices skyrocket and Sony adapts by killing physical discs . Change is afoot, with traditional triple-A games looking more and more like a potential casualty. No one is safe and nothing is sacred. Not even a seemingly unshakable pillar like Warframe .

“It’s fucking awful, what’s happening in this industry,” community director Megan Everett told Aftermath during a recent interview. “I feel very lucky to be working on this game and to have this dev team and the community that we’ve built, because I’m no stranger to the fact that it could all end tomorrow. Games as a service, it's a scary world. We've been doing it for 13 years, and we have no secret formula. We have no cheat code. We have just stayed in our little Canadian homebrew lane, put our heads down, and really tried to make this game what we want and what our community wants it to be.”

For developers at Digital Extremes, that has at various points meant eschewing trends and sticking to their many, many guns.

"I think back to when PUBG and Fortnite were popping off,” said Everett, “and everyone was making a battle royale game, and I remember internally we all looked at each other like 'We're not doing that.’"

"That's not what our expertise is, and we know where our strengths are,” Everett continued. “We know where we can stray a little bit and get wacky and weird, and so we do that. But it would be a disservice if we went so crazy that [we lost sight of Warframe 's identity]. Like, we've tried the PvP thing; we've tried Conclave. It didn't work, and we know that's not what we're about, so we shifted to focusing on the story and the narrative. I think that has been a good thing for us in that we know where our strengths are."

The TennoCon Tau demo leaned into those narrative proclivities, with cinematic flourishes aplenty. An intro to the new solar system that—if you’re a longtime Warframe player—was frankly chill-inducing gave way to a perspective switch to an all-new Warfame, Brysko, a creation of Albrecht Entrati, the Void-harnessing scientist who set much of Warframe ’s story into motion. Brysko’s whole thing is that he’s basically an old-timey noir detective in Warframe form. Lacking a mouth to call his own, he monologues about everything that happens to him in his head—which, of course, the player can hear, even though no one else can.

But why a noir detective to introduce a new solar system? Because there are mysteries afoot in both the cosmic and more immediate senses. And also because Ford really liked Law And Order as a kid (Brysko = Briscoe, by the way) and Blade Runner as an adult.

"I wanted [those inspirations] in Warframe ,” said Ford. “It's kind of that juvenile, but I wanted Warframe to explore gangs, addiction, [and] lifespan of constructs. All of these things are super relevant to Sentients, who as a construct were created by man—no different than androids. So this was essential for the tone of Tau's first reveal."

The idea of a monologuing Warframe followed shortly after.

"Credit to Adrian, our lead writer, because we were trying to think of how to go noir and tell the world of Tau from a perspective that needs to feel new, and he really put his heart out on his sleeve and was like 'I really think we could get an inner monologue working for this,”' said Ford. “We proved it within a day. We were like 'That's it, that has to be it.' Because nothing else would work in the world with the pieces we put on the chessboard. It's too bleak, it's too dark, it's too weird, and the unknowable inner monologue of this character felt very extreme, but in my heart and soul, I feel like it works. And if it works for at least just me, then you know what? Happy early Christmas to me."

What ensued in the TennoCon demo was a stroll through a grimy, goopy noir slum—if H.R. Giger was in the business of imagining such places. Sentients, addicted to a drug called Bloom, writhed and in some cases attacked as Brysko waxed nihilistic about their dead-end lives in the universe’s rock bottom. “All dreaming or dead,” he said of the Sentients at one point. “Until the rains come.” But in his cheery estimation, things could always be worse: “At least I’m not out on one of the old battlefields,” he growled mid-combat, voiced by the inimitable Matt Mercer, who made the hammy dialogue a lot more convincing. “Warframes and Sentients lurching back to life, fighting old wars over and over again. Mindless. Heartless. Dead.”

items.title: Warframe is showing love to tabletop games with this ‘legally distinct’ RPG
items.summary: Amir finally gets to play Fables & Frontiers this August
items.content:
By Simone De Rochefort Published Jul 11, 2026, 6:01 PM EDT Gaming Amir finally gets to play Fables & Frontiers this August

All the new Warframe and Soulframe announcements at TennoCon 2026 All the new Warframe and Soulframe announcements at TennoCon 2026 You can play Warframe’s fantasy successor, Soulframe, for free right now Warframe is showing love to tabletop games with this ‘legally distinct’ RPG Image: Digital Extremes Warframe Sign in to your Polygon.com account Dungeons & Dragons is in the air at Digital Extremes. The developer is introducing a new minigame to Warframe : a “legally distinct” roleplaying game called Fables & Frontiers .

Longtime players will recognize the name. It’s the fantasy tabletop game that Amir, one of the 1999 expansion protoframes, desperately wants to play with the rest of the Hex. Well, it’s finally time for Amir’s dream of playing a game with his friends to come true.

“This particular mini adventure is staying forever as a permanent expansion to the Hex,” Warframe creative director Rebecca Ford told Polygon in a pre-TennoCon 2026 interview. “What we found was as we move away from 1999 , year over year, it's just too important to leave.”

Warframe: 1999 expanded the human cast of the game for the first time, introducing protoframes that hold the line for humanity against an alien infestation. Also, you can kiss them . Now the game’s narrative is pushing toward a new star system with the upcoming Tau expansion, but the protoframes are beloved characters whose stories still matter to the community.

Image: Digital Extremes “People for a long time have been like, ‘When is Amir ever gonna play [...] Fables & Frontiers ’?” said community director Megan Everett. “And so I think this is a nice little community love letter.”

A dedicated space in the Höllvania Mall will be redecorated as Amir’s F&F campaign room, complete with maps, game pieces, and the ability to summon Amir in a flash. The minigame itself is a text-based adventure that happens in the K.I.M. messaging system, which players already use to build relationships with the Hex.

Fables & Frontiers became a real, playable minigame within Warframe because tabletop roleplaying is not a fad for the development team at Digital Extremes.

“It's been a lifelong hobby for a lot of us,” said Ford. “We used to play Pathfinder , Dungeons & Dragons , and then it became a community team thing.”

Fables & Frontiers was included as a reference in the 1999 expansion as a gesture of “verisimilitude,” said Ford. “And then the campaign idea came together when we said we wanna do a Nightwave that is specifically focused on 1999, give it some flavor,” she said.

Image: Digital Extremes Fittingly then, the free Nightwave battle pass system will be taken over by Amir this August, in an event called Amir’s Shockwave. Shockwave will feature themed daily and weekly quests with new rewards — one of which is the new On-Lyne song, “Running Late.” It’s against this backdrop that players will be able to enjoy Fables & Frontiers .

The D&D flavor doesn’t end there, though, because acclaimed DM and voice actor Matthew Mercer is joining the Warframe cast with the Tau expansion, as the detective-themed Warframe Brysko.

items.title: Warframe players, it's time to become a Banshee main again
items.summary: The sound-based Warframe Banshee will be revamped with the fall 2026 update, Iceblade of Narin, Digital Extremes announced at TennoCon 2026
items.content:
By Simone De Rochefort Published Jul 11, 2026, 6:01 PM EDT News It's Banshee's time to shine

All the new Warframe and Soulframe announcements at TennoCon 2026 All the new Warframe and Soulframe announcements at TennoCon 2026 You can play Warframe’s fantasy successor, Soulframe, for free right now Everyone’s least favorite Warframe is getting revamped Warframe Sign in to your Polygon.com account One of Warframe ’s oldest playable Warframes is finally getting a much-needed new kit.

Since Warframe ’s debut in 2013 , it has transformed from a methodical and monochrome shooter into a riotous fast-paced space opera. The roster of playable Warframes has, in turn, grown with increasingly complex abilities.

Banshee, who debuted in the game’s first year, is now getting a mechanical makeover. A sound-based Warframe, her current kit has a focus on crowd control and allows her to push, stagger, and stun enemies with sonic powers. She can also apply a debuff that appears as a highlighted “weak spot.”

“It used to be the best kit in the game due to how important crowd control was,” said Warframe creative director Rebecca Ford. But in the 13 years since Warframe launched, a parade of new Warframes have joined the fray.

“Think of all the warframes that have come out since 2013 that touched that crowd control itch and, you know, are just as complicated as they get now in 2026,” said community director Megan Everett. Aside from the new Warframes, the team at Digital Extremes has also added mods that players can use to increase weak-point damage and emphasized area-of-effect abilities with Blast damage.

“It was time to put all of that into practice with the original crowd control and damage scaling frame that rewarded accuracy. So that's very much the thesis of her touch up,” said Ford.

As one of the game’s oldest Warframes, Banshee has dedicated fans among the player base who have been clamoring for a rework. Digital Extremes frequently updates older frames to bring their abilities more in line with the ever-changing game.

“For a good amount of time now, the actual community has been like, ‘Please, Banshee, please,’” said Everett. “We were just at Anime Expo , and someone literally stood up and said, "Please rework Banshee." And we're like, "Oh!”

IMAGE: Digital Extremes Digital Extremes regularly takes the community’s pulse through Devstreams and events like TennoCon.

“We have kind of like a running roster of what the community are actually, like, hungry for,” said Everett. “Banshee has been one of the top of the list for a good amount of time now… she is due.”

Banshee’s rework will be available as part of Warframe ’s fall update, Iceblade of Narin, alongside Deluxe skins for Banshee and Qorvex, a new ice-themed Warframe, and Citrine Prime.

items.title: Warframe: Tau Breaks Beyond the Origin System After a Decade, Sending Tenno to a Rain-Soaked Criminal City Called Fornax
items.summary: Warframe: Tau sends the Tenno to the Tau System and Fornax, a rain-soaked Sentient city, with Albrecht Entrati. It's out later in 2026.
items.content:
TennoCon 2026 delivered another big Warframe reveal by Digital Extremes , headlined by a brand-new Narrative Chapter that pushes the game beyond the Origin System for the first time, a new Chimera Warframe voiced by Matthew Mercer , and a full slate of cosmetic, seasonal, and endgame content confirmed for the rest of 2026.

Warframe's next major Narrative Chapter, Tau , sends the Tenno to an entirely new Star Chart called (you guessed it) the Tau System, marking the first time the game has expanded beyond its long-running Origin System setting. The chapter launches free on all platforms later this year, with the initial release centered on Fornax , a Sentient ring city, and at least one additional unrevealed location.

Fornax is depicted as a decaying, rain-soaked criminal metropolis where black rain corrodes both the environment and its inhabitants' minds, and where a highly addictive substance called Bloom fuels a citywide drug economy. Long-term addicts are shown as Bloom roots, eating away at people who no longer care because the drug numbs the psychological toll of the black rain. The main quest has players working with Albrecht Entrati , who awaits the Tenno's return, to investigate the fallen city and its criminal power structure. The primary antagonist is The Hunra , a ruthless crime boss voiced by Jonathan Bullock , who controls Fornax through Bloom production and a network of enforcers and smugglers. Developers described Hunra as a title rather than a proper name, suggesting the position itself may carry weight beyond the individual currently holding it.

The Tau chapter introduces Brysko , a Chimera Warframe rather than a standard Warframe or Protoframe: he was specifically engineered by Entrati to serve as a trusted infiltration unit inside The Hunra's criminal circle ahead of the Tenno's arrival. Brysko is silent to the outside world but has a full inner monologue voiced by Matthew Mercer (famed for his work in The Legend of Vox Machina and Final Fantasy VII Rebirth, among many others), marking the actor's first work in the Warframe universe.

In the demo, Brysko poses as a low-level enforcer running errands for The Hunra while secretly feeding intelligence back to Entrati, risking violent retaliation if his double-dealing is discovered. He is shown carrying out enforcement jobs against small-time smugglers on Entrati's orders, all while building toward direct access to The Hunra's inner circle.

Combat-wise, Brysko wields a new brass-knuckle melee weapon called Rain & Shine, explosive playing cards as a secondary tool, and an Exalted revolver magnum named the Corecracker. He will ship as the Warframe bundled directly with the Tau update.

Fornax's criminal underworld adds Portau , Warframe's first-ever card mini-game, built on poker mechanics but reworked with Sentient-themed suits called Blooms, Cores, Moons, and Suns. The game is portrayed as wildly popular in The Hunra's gambling dens, offering players a new social and risk-based activity directly tied to the Tau setting.

Fornax also introduces a distinct new Sentient enemy faction called the Fornax Drowners , described as Sentients that have evolved differently from those players have fought in the Origin System. The environment mixes high-end casinos, grimy shipping docks, and dilapidated slums, and the demo shows Warframes and Sentients seemingly reanimating on old battlefields to endlessly refight past wars, described in the demo as mindless, heartless, dead combatants stuck reliving old conflicts.

Despite the pivot to Tau, developers directly addressed concerns that Warframe is abandoning its existing setting, confirming that deep new story beats for the Origin System are still planned and ready to go . The team emphasized they intend to keep both worlds alive rather than treating Tau as a full replacement for existing content.

For players jumping into Warframe for the first time or returning after a long break, developers pointed to the in-game Tenno Tracker system as the best way to catch up. Following it end-to-end effectively delivers a guided campaign experience culminating in readiness for the Tau content, letting new players treat it as a structured story-first path through the game's sprawling narrative

Starting in August, Nightwave is getting a themed takeover called Shockwave , hosted by Protoframe Amir (Volt), tied to the Warframe: 1999 storyline and the long-teased "Fables and Frontiers" concept involving Amir and the Hex crew. The event expands the in-game K.I.M. messaging system with a new permanent group chat feature and a walkable mini-adventure.

Developers stressed that the mini-adventure and its setup will remain in the game permanently, so only the reward track itself is time-limited to the Nightwave event window. Rewards include Hex-themed glyphs created by community artist Zildy , and the long-awaited third song from in-game boy band Onlyne, titled "Running Late."

Last but not least, Digital Extremes officially named the Warframe Fall 2026 update: Iceblade of Narin . Confirmed contents include:

The studio claimed Iceblade of Narin will target players roughly at the " post Angels of the Zariman " progression point, making it a mid-to-late-game update rather than early-game content. Full details are planned to be shared in a devstream that will air on September 4 in Seoul, South Korea (the studio's first-ever event in the country).

items.title: Warframe's Next Expansion Is the Epic Payoff to 13 Years of Storytelling
items.summary: Warframe Tau might be Digital Extremes' version of Bungie's Destiny 2 expansion The Final Shape.
items.content:
Over the past few years, Warframe players have spent much of their time in-game exploring a broken timestream, jumping from one era to the next in their fight against an eldritch space god .

But at its TennoCon 2026 event, developer Digital Extremes revealed a bold new direction for the game: Instead of exploring time, players will soon enter an entirely new part of outer space. The Tau solar system teased in The Old Peace update will soon be accessible during regular gameplay, with multiple new celestial bodies for players to explore.

Live-service games that thrive for years get the rare chance to morph and evolve into something new. When Warframe launched in 2013, it was a simple third-person looter-shooter about deadly robotic ninjas. Now it features raid bosses, an endless roguelike mode, a semi-open-world fishing simulator and even some visual novel elements .

In an industry that seems increasingly reluctant to take risks on new ideas, Digital Extremes continues to reinvent its sci-fi brainchild. Each new update includes creative leaps leading to "out-there" ideas like a living guitar or a spider-themed robot , ensuring there's no other game quite like Warframe.

Even so, all those zany ideas existed within a single solar system -- disparate story threads connected by a common location. The boundaries of the world have always been well-defined by the in-game star chart. In the next expansion, that's changing for the first time ever.

Leaving behind the Origin System will introduce players to worlds that are more alien than they've ever seen before.

Warframe players have been hacking, slashing and bullet-jumping across the Origin System -- a starchart that mirrors our real-world solar system with some sci-fi alterations -- for over 13 years. Soon it'll be time to explore a new frontier: Tau.

It's difficult to express how massive this change feels without pointing toward the other sci-fi looter-shooter cultural juggernaut, Bungie's Destiny and Destiny 2.

The Destiny series -- first released in 2014, just one year after Warframe -- also took place largely within the boundaries of our very own solar system. For years, players battled the forces of darkness across the surface of Earth, Mars and other familiar planets.

But when Destiny 2's decade-long narrative reached its climax in The Final Shape expansion , players plunged into the heart of the living planet-like Traveler in order to battle the villainous Witness. When the stakes were highest, players were transported to a completely alien locale within the deity-like sphere they'd followed for Destiny's entire plot, a place where everyone was on equal footing and many long-standing questions were finally answered.

Warframe Tau looks like it'll provide answers to some longstanding questions -- like what deal the Lotus struck with the eldritch Man in the Wall.

While Warframe's narrative has flirted with the idea of sending players to Tau for years now, the reality of finally heading to a brand new star chart feels monumental.

This could be Warframe's version of The Final Shape -- and while players may not be putting the beatdown on this game's eldritch god quite yet, this is Digital Extremes' chance to build out an expansion to its sci-fi universe that is completely divorced from the imagery and cultural norms associated with our real world. Warframe is already wild, but the Tau system presents an opportunity to explore ideas unbound by the established conventions of the Origin System.

That's not to say everything will be set in stone when Tau comes to the live build. The new star system will be slowly built out after this initial narrative adventure, which features a distinctly detective-noir vibe, naturally paired with a fedora-wearing, smooth-talking Warframe named Brysko.

items.title: Warframe lead Rebecca Ford says Destiny 2's death is "horrible news" for everyone: "Not the first time it's happened, and it'll happen again"
items.content:
"The idea that we aren't in charge of our own goodbye is something I wake up thinking about every single day"

When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

(Image credit: Digital Extremes) Copy link Facebook X Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Warframe leadership isn't about to celebrate the impending death of Destiny 2, even if they are on course to outlive one of their game's strongest competitors in the sci-fi live-service space.

Developer Bungie announced in May that it's ending active development on Destiny 2 after more than a decade as it turns its attention toward its new extraction shooter Marathon . Destiny 2 will remain playable for the foreseeable future, but its briefly resurgent Monument of Triumph update from June will be its last major content drop as static events and retouched modes sustain the game's live operations.

Warframe, meanwhile, which released a year before Destiny (the original) in 2013, is showing no signs of slowing down with major story and content updates continuing at a regular cadence. Still, creative director Rebecca Ford refuses to dance on a grave, not just because she's above something like that, but because she knows that if a game as enduring and influential as Destiny 2 can fall in favor of short-term business needs, anything can.

"It's horrible news, because it shows that even if you care so much, the business side of this industry always gets the last remark," Ford tells GamesRadar+ during a conversation at TennoCon 2026.

"Those are the types of stories and experiences that, when you're in a position where you have your own game, your own IP, and you work as hard as you do on it... That's not the first time it's happened, and it'll happen again, where the business aspect of the video game economy makes the decision for you, and it is existentially threatening at every level, because the idea that we aren't in charge of our own goodbye is something I wake up thinking about every single day."

Warframe leads join fans mourning Destiny 2: "There is no Warframe without the legacy of Bungie" Destiny 2's end is "earth-shattering," Warframe dev says, despite the competition between the 2 shooters As Destiny 2 waves goodbye in its final update, Warframe commemorates the Bungie MMO with an in-game reward Unless you're a strictly independent, private studio which doesn't answer to shareholders, this is the sort of thing every game developer worries about to some degree in 2026 as consolidation, mass layoffs, studio closures, and game cancelations define one of the most turbulent periods in games industry history. Everything happening at Xbox this week spells nothing but bad news for devs and the projects which now may or may not ever come to light.

Case in point: The Elder Scrolls 6 will be hurt and potentially delayed by Xbox layoffs, Bethesda devs expect: "This has had a crushing effect on morale"

Weekly digests, tales from the communities you love, and more

After earning an English degree from ASU, I worked as a corporate copy editor while freelancing for places like SFX Magazine, Screen Rant, Game Revolution, and MMORPG on the side. I got my big break here in 2019 with a freelance news gig, and I was hired on as GamesRadar's west coast Staff Writer in 2021. That means I'm responsible for managing the site's western regional executive branch, AKA my home office, and writing about whatever horror game I'm too afraid to finish. items.title: TennoCon 2026 Relay Experience
items.summary: This year's TennoCon Relay Experience is now available to join! Jump in-game and head to the TennoLive 2026 Relay orbiting Saturn. Once you arrive, head straight to the Dry Dock and get ready for the show to begin at 4:30 p.m. ET. Please no
items.content: This year's TennoCon Relay Experience is now available to join! Jump in-game and head to the TennoLive 2026 Relay orbiting Saturn. Once you arrive, head straight to the Dry Dock and get ready for the show to begin at 4:30 p.m. ET. Please note: in order to access the Relay you must have completed at least one Mission on the Star Chart. See you there, Tenno!

The legendary gunslinger receives a bounty of new Customizations through the new Mesa Heirloom Collection, featuring her signature brimmed Signa and travel-worn Duster attachments to further adjust the loadout to your liking. items.title: Titania Prime and Gara Prime Enter Prime Resurgence
items.summary: Dominate your enemies through diminutive fae mischief or shatter their corporeal form with Prime Resurgence! Add Titania Prime and Gara Prime, along with their signature Prime Weapons and Prime Accessories, to your Arsenal today with Regal
items.content:
Dominate your enemies through diminutive fae mischief or shatter their corporeal form with Prime Resurgence! Add Titania Prime and Gara Prime, along with their signature Prime Weapons and Prime Accessories, to your Arsenal today with Regal Aya.

Titania Prime

From a flowery bed to the clouds above, a fae queen blissfully invites her foes to tumble down again. Titania Prime deploys legions of butterflies to disorient and dominate any adversary, making her an excellent pick for those looking for a Warframe with strong crowd control capabilities. Razorwing, Titania Prime’s 4th Ability, even allows her to shrink down and take flight, becoming part of the mystifying swarm she commands.

Gara Prime

Even the faintest touch from Gara Prime is enough to shatter both mind and body of any that stand in her way. This formidable glassblower is able to reshape the battlefield through molten crystal that can splinter enemies while also bolstering defenses of allies. Gara Prime’s high survivability and aptitude for significant damage output should be appealing to anyone who has no interest in being a glass cannon.

WHAT ARE PRIMES?

When you require the finest and most powerful armaments, only Primes will do. Exquisitely gilded, every Prime Warframe, Weapon and item exists in its purest form — just as the Orokin intended. If you're new to Warframe, you can learn more about the Prime Resurgence Program by visiting our official FAQs page and watching our community video guide below:

items.title: Warframe of Mind: In praise of Warframe’s optimistic nihilism
items.content:
I had originally planned a very different column to run today, and it would have been part of Vague Patch Notes , but then Microsoft happened. It was a more serious column, and for a moment I considered writing a column about that, but I found that it just kept devolving into articulate but not terribly interesting rage. I don’t need to tell you that Microsoft is bad and has mismanaged its core business in a way that results in people losing their jobs, and I don’t need to explain to you that the people who are harmed by these decisions aren’t the people making them. Either you know all this or you are actively resistant to the realization.

No, what I want to talk about this week is something different because as we’re gearing up for TennoCon and as I thought about the sort of media that’s actually a palliative response to this kind of awfulness, yes, I thought about Warframe . It’s a game about saving the galaxy… except it’s not about that because you already start too late for the galaxy to be saved in any meaningful fashion, and there’s an almost terrifying hope and love that flows from that.

You might think that I’m just doing a bit because one of the reveals of 1999 was that the universe of Warframe was never really our universe. But that’s honestly not what I meant. In fact, the reveal that this was never the same as our timeline is actually an important point underscoring the positivity I just mentioned. Because as soon as the game starts up, from the moment you are granted control over your first frame, you are taught one thing: The Origin System is a mess.

The Tenno killed the Orokin, and that was good, but in the intervening time, things haven’t gotten better but worse . The Grineer are a giant, thrashing beast that exists just to make war against people who can barely fight back. The Corpus are an omnicorporation with no purpose, chasing credits as a means to an end. The Infestation rages, it isn’t being controlled, and the closest thing anyone has to a seal on the Infestation is barely held together by a bickering family.

But when you peel back the layers on all of that, you find out that… actually, it’s all much worse than any of that implies.

Sitting beneath everything is the Void, with the Man in the Wall sitting at the heart and very clearly manipulating events for some purpose we don’t know. And that the heart of what happened to our player character and informs so many indescribable hells that have been manufactured. The Hex are literally trapped in a time loop that they are aware of only enough to know that it exists and that we can reset it as many times as we want , and the Hex are our allies .

That’s kind of the recurring theme in Warframe . It’s all bad. And once you learn how bad it is, it gets worse. And far more importantly? You can’t fix it.

I mean, sure, you can do something. You’re a Tenno; you are personally more than a match for entire platoons of Grineer. But the thing is that the Grineer have, functionally, infinite platoons. You can stop this one and maybe the next dozen, but you can’t stop all of them. You cannot institutionally wreck the Grineer. The Tenno, as a faction, do not have the numbers or strength to really cause a full-scale revolution that would take out the most material threats to life in the Origin System… and that’s just the basic stuff, never mind the Sentients still roaming.

This is part of why 1999 specifically makes it clear how different the world was even then because it’s part of the point. The Origin System is very screwed up and in need of saving, but the thing is that even if you could somehow reverse time, you would not be faced with a familiar world that you, the player, recognize. Even in 1999, the problems were there. The Techrot had happened, and it would spread, and further problems would arise.

Welcome to Warframe . The world is fucked up, and it is well beyond the point when any of your actions are going to make it not fucked up. It’s all chaotic, everything is broken and messy, and you cannot turn it back into a version of the world you recognize. All of the damage has been done and cannot be undone. The end of the world happened, and then after it was done pulverizing things into dust, it came back around for seconds. You are not going to make this all right.

How can I call this uplifting? Because the game doesn’t end there. The game starts there. It tells you that the world is like this, and then it hands you a gun and asks, “So what are you going to do, Tenno?”

Video games like to tell you to save the world. Warframe does not. You cannot save the world. But you can save something right here. It’s why Duviri’s story ends with the Drifter deciding that saving a life is more important than escape. It’s why Deimos has you pulling a fractured, angry family back together at great cost. It’s why Onkko sits just behind Konzu and Saya, his ex-wife and his best friend, willing to let her think he’s dead and for the two of them to be together because he’d rather have her be happy than do what might make him happiest.

Because sometimes the world is like that. It breaks and you can’t fix it. Something changes beneath your feet, and now you have to find a path forward you never expected to deal with. It sucks, and the temptation is always there to lie down… but the game’s narrative goes the next step.

You can make the world a better place. You can have a tangible impact, even if it’s not a big enough impact to fix the world. You can hammer one corner of the galaxy into a better shape. Even if you can’t pull the Hex out of the loop, you can be a friend to the members trapped, and you can hope that you’ll find a way eventually. Roathe cannot make up for the things he did that he knows were wrong, but he can bring Loid up to reconnect with the family.
